title: Neural-map correlation field: localization becomes sliding-window matching of a crop fingerprint against a learned 32 m map raster
hypothesis: The last five reverted experiments (26-30: 3x trunk capacity, coverage calibration, stride-8 readout tap, lighting-specialist dispatch) all attacked what feeds or routes around the SAME output parameterization -- a flat table of 1,024 independent ~217 m cell templates scored by dot product against one holistic 560-d crop vector, unchanged since exp 2 -- and were jointly refuted. Per the plateau rule this iteration replaces that parameterization itself, the one design family CLAUDE.md §3 invites ('a different coordinate parameterization') that the history has never touched. Three signatures say the template table is the binding constraint: (1) since exps 20/25 the converged model's train-split and eval-split medians are EQUAL (~600-800 m) -- it cannot even fit visited locations, the signature of a statistically weak output parameterization, not of missing capacity (exps 26/28 proved bigger trunks don't score) or missing optimization (exp 25); (2) rural prignitz, with distinctive macro-structure, beats all three self-similar metros in every bucket -- lookalike-cell confusion, which holistic-vector template scoring cannot resolve because it enforces no spatial-arrangement consistency between crop and cell; (3) mean/median ~= 2 everywhere -- a heavy tail of long-range aliasing errors, the same confusion. The change (OrienterNet-family matching, encoded entirely in weights -- no reference imagery ships): delete the 1,024-template tables and learn a NEURAL MAP, an 8-channel raster over the area at ~32 m/cell (dimensions derived from meta.json width/height -- no area-specific constants), scoring every raster position by cross-correlating a crop-derived 4x4x8 feature kernel whose taps each summarize 32 m of ground, matching the raster's cell size. One mechanism addresses all three signatures: (a) a position scores high only if the crop's whole 4x4 spatial arrangement matches the map there, so lookalike districts differing in sub-crop layout are suppressed geometrically; (b) the scoring function is shared across all ~45k positions (one matching kernel + one map) instead of 1,024 separately-learned discriminants, and overlapping training crops write coherently into the SAME map cells at their true offsets, multiplying effective supervision per output parameter; (c) the decode grid drops from 217 m to ~32 m pitch. The kept exp-12 bright/dark expert pair and gate survive as two kernel generators (layout-aware conv vs GAP-only linear) blended before a single correlation -- correlation is linear in the kernel, so blending kernels equals blending fields; the C4 vote (exp 16), beta-sharpened decode (exp 14), shape-feature confidence + calibration (exp 15), fresh-draw sampler (exp 20), 3-realization renders (exp 17) and 24-epoch cosine schedule (exp 25) are all byte-identical, and supervision smoothness is preserved exactly (Gaussian-CE sigma stays 1.5/32 in normalized map units).
method: model/model.py: remove loc_logits (560->1024), dark_logits (48->1024) and the GRID_K template machinery. Add per-area parameters map_feat [8, mh, mw] (mh=round(height/32), mw=round(width/32), init 0.01*randn) and map_bias [(mh-3)*(mw-3)] (init 0). The trunk's 8x8x48 fmap is projected by a 1x1 conv (48->8) and 2x2 avg-pooled into a 4x4x8 'bright' kernel; a Linear(48->128) forms a 'dark' kernel from the GAP descriptor; the existing lum+GAP gate blends the two KERNELS per example; logits4 = F.conv2d(map_feat.unsqueeze(0), blended_kernels).squeeze(0).flatten(1) + map_bias -- valid cross-correlation, one logit per raster position, C4-averaged exactly as before. Cell centers: u=(j+2)/mw, v=(i+2)/mh, row-major. Decode, confidence features, and loss keep their exact form with TARGET_SIGMA_UV = 1.5/32. model/train.py: build_model(meta) passes area dims; loss_fn additionally receives the model's cell-center buffers. Sampler, calibration, schedule, CLI, export contract untouched.
expected_outcome: If arrangement-enforcing matching plus map-space parameter sharing is the fix the last five refutations jointly point at, worst-case median drops 10-25% below the 797.91 m champion (predict 600-720 m), with metros gaining more than prignitz (lookalike suppression) and mean/median falling toward 1.5 (fewer long-range aliases); the finer 32 m grid alone is worth little at current error levels. Confidence machinery is untouched, so per-bucket coverage should stay near the champion's 0.31-0.52 profile -- a median gain achieved at collapsed coverage would not count (coverage-median coupling). Named failure mode: after the C4 vote a residual heading mismatch up to +/-45 deg misaligns outer kernel taps by ~1 map cell; if learned features cannot absorb that, correlation peaks smear, fields flatten, and errors move toward the ~3.2 km center-guess floor with coverage collapse. Deployment: export ~2.8-3.0 MB fp32 (< 4 MiB gate, no int8 needed), host latency estimated < 40 ms (< 250 ms gate).
init_strategy: pretrained:mobilenet_v3_small IMAGENET1K_V1 features[0..8] (trunk unchanged); neural map + kernel heads from scratch
eli5: Until now the model answered 'where am I?' by comparing one overall impression of the camera frame against about a thousand memorized district flashcards -- and in a city full of lookalike districts, overall impressions collide. Now the model instead paints its own miniature map of the whole area into its weights while training. In flight it turns the camera frame into a small 4x4 fingerprint tile and slides that tile across its painted map to find where the pattern truly lines up, the way you find where a puzzle piece belongs. Because the whole arrangement inside the fingerprint has to line up -- not just the general vibe -- two neighborhoods that merely feel similar no longer fool it, and the answer grid becomes about seven times finer in each direction.

stages.name: Camera frame
stages.detail: 128×128 px aerial crop, one of 6 simulated lighting conditions

stages.name: Rotation fan
stages.detail: the crop is copied into its four 90° turns (exact pixel re-arrangements, no blur) and all four copies travel through the same network side by side

stages.name: Feature extractor
stages.detail: ImageNet-pretrained MobileNetV3-Small (first 9 blocks, 190k params), gently fine-tuned

stages.name: Crop fingerprint
stages.detail: REPLACES the layout summary: the 8×8 feature grid is condensed into a small 4×4 fingerprint tile (each square summarizing a 32 m patch of ground); a second fingerprint is formed from the 48-number texture average alone, for dark frames

stages.name: Lighting gate
stages.detail: a tiny network reads the crop's own average brightness together with the 48-number texture summary and outputs one number: how night-like is this shot? (the gate itself is unchanged — it now blends the two fingerprint tiles instead of two finished heat maps)

stages.name: Probability map
stages.detail: REPLACES the flashcard scorer: the model carries a learned miniature map of the whole area in its own weights (8 numbers per ~32 m map square, sized from the area's dimensions) and slides the fingerprint tile across it — a position only scores high when the whole 4×4 arrangement lines up — giving a heat map with ~45,000 positions at 32 m spacing instead of 1,024 cells at 217 m; the four turned copies are still averaged into one voted map

stages.name: Decode
stages.detail: the heat map's contrast is turned up first (peaks boosted, faint background suppressed), then the answer is the balance point of the sharpened map — committing to the strongest hotspot instead of averaging every faint guess across the whole city

stages.name: Confidence
stages.detail: the model inspects the shape of its own heat map — one crisp hotspot means sure, a washed-out smear means unsure — and below a tuned bar it says 'no fix' instead of guessing; the bar is set so it still answers at least 40% of the time in every lighting condition

stages.name: Training signal
stages.detail: unchanged — same position and confidence grading as before (same Gaussian tolerance in meters), now graded on the finer 32 m answer grid

stages.name: Training data
stages.detail: unchanged — every pass draws a fresh set of 6,000 places per lighting condition (new headings, new simulator-roll mix), so distinct places are seen only once or twice each and memorizing individual crops doesn't pay

stages.name: Training schedule
stages.detail: unchanged — 24 fresh-draw passes with the cosine glide kept from round 5

implementation_brief:
Two files change: model/model.py and model/train.py. Nothing else. Do not touch pipeline/, FROZEN files, or anything hamburg.

FIXED CONTRACTS (restated, must survive verbatim):
- train.py CLI: python -m model.train --area A --out-dir D [--data-dir P] [--epochs N] [--max-crops-per-bucket M] [--seed S]; exports ONNX to <out-dir>/models/<area>.onnx and appends an info dict to <out-dir>/train_info.json.
- ONNX contract (model.py docstring): input 1x3x128x128 float32 in [0,1], output [[u, v, conf]] with u,v normalized map coords and conf in [0,1]; opset 17, dynamo=False. The frozen scorer runs this ONNX single-threaded: file must be <= 4 MiB, host latency <= 250 ms.

=== model/model.py ===
1. Constants: delete GRID_K and TARGET_SIGMA_CELLS; add MAP_CELL_PX = 32 (map raster cell size in reference-image pixels), KERNEL_K = 4, MAP_CH = 8, and TARGET_SIGMA_UV = 1.5 / 32.0 (identical supervision width to the champion, now stated in normalized uv). Keep DECODE_BETA = 3.0, GOOD_ERR_UV = 0.05, pretrained path, ImageNet mean/std, _build_pretrained_trunk, _c4_stack unchanged.
2. Replace _grid_centers with _map_cell_centers(mh, mw, k=KERNEL_K): returns flat float32 tensors of length n_pos=(mh-k+1)*(mw-k+1), row-major over positions (i, j) with i in [0, mh-k], j in [0, mw-k]: cell_u[idx]=(j + k/2)/mw, cell_v[idx]=(i + k/2)/mh, idx = i*(mw-k+1)+j. This ordering MUST match flatten(1) of a [.., H', W'] conv output.
3. TinyLocNet.__init__(self, map_hw: tuple[int, int], gate_hidden: int = 16): keep self.features, norm buffers, gate (Linear(48+1,16), ReLU, Linear(16,1)), conf_head (Linear(48+3,32), ReLU, Linear(32,1)), conf_shift buffer exactly as today. Remove layout_squeeze, loc_logits, dark_logits. Add: mh, mw = map_hw; self.kernel_proj = nn.Conv2d(48, MAP_CH, 1); self.dark_kernel = nn.Linear(48, MAP_CH * KERNEL_K * KERNEL_K); self.map_feat = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(MAP_CH, mh, mw) * 0.01); self.map_bias = nn.Parameter(torch.zeros((mh-KERNEL_K+1) * (mw-KERNEL_K+1))); register cell_u/cell_v buffers from _map_cell_centers; store self.n_pos.
4. forward(x, return_logits=False): identical through lum, x4=_c4_stack(x), fmap=self.features(normalized x4), f4=fmap.mean((2,3)), lum4=lum.repeat(4,1). Then: kb4 = F.avg_pool2d(self.kernel_proj(fmap), 2)  # [4N, 8, 4, 4]; kd4 = self.dark_kernel(f4).view(-1, MAP_CH, KERNEL_K, KERNEL_K); g4 = torch.sigmoid(self.gate(torch.cat([lum4, f4], 1)));  k4 = (1 - g4).view(-1,1,1,1) * kb4 + g4.view(-1,1,1,1) * kd4; logits4 = F.conv2d(self.map_feat.unsqueeze(0), k4).squeeze(0).flatten(1) + self.map_bias.unsqueeze(0)  # [4N, n_pos] -- conv2d with per-sample kernels as out_channels over a batch-1 input IS the valid sliding cross-correlation. Then exactly as today: logits = logits4.reshape(4, n, -1).mean(0); f = f4.reshape(4, n, -1).mean(0); p = softmax(DECODE_BETA*logits); u,v = expectations against cell_u/cell_v; p1/u1/v1 unsharpened; peak/ent/gap conf features with ent normalized by log(self.n_pos); conf = sigmoid(conf_head(...) - conf_shift); return cat + optional logits.
5. loss_fn(pred, logits, target_uv, cell_u, cell_v): same three terms with identical weights. du = cell_u[None,:] - target_uv[:,0:1]; dv likewise; g = exp(-(du**2+dv**2)/(2*TARGET_SIGMA_UV**2)); g /= g.sum(1,keepdim=True); ce = -(g*log_softmax(logits,1)).sum(1).mean(); coord L2 and conf BCE (GOOD_ERR_UV, weight 0.3) byte-identical to today.
6. build_model(meta: dict) -> nn.Module: mh = round(meta['height']/MAP_CELL_PX); mw = round(meta['width']/MAP_CELL_PX); return TinyLocNet((mh, mw)).
7. export_onnx / estimate_position unchanged. The primary conv2d path is ALREADY PROVEN: runs/_probe_iter11_corr_export.py exported this exact head (berlin- and munich-sized rasters) at 2.16-2.40 MB / ~4 ms single-thread with a 25k-param stand-in stem -- with the real 190k trunk expect ~2.8-3.1 MB, inside both gates. FALLBACK ONLY IF the full model still trips export: replace the F.conv2d line with 16 statically-unrolled shifted matmuls -- for dy in range(4): for dx in range(4): logits4 += k4[:, :, dy, dx] @ self.map_feat[:, dy:dy+mh-3, dx:dx+mw-3].reshape(MAP_CH, -1) -- and pass do_constant_folding=False to torch.onnx.export (otherwise the 16 folded map slices are materialized as 16 near-full copies of the raster and blow the 4 MiB gate).
8. Append a short exp-31 paragraph to the module docstring describing the neural-map correlation head, keeping the exported-contract sentences intact.

=== model/train.py ===
9. train_area: meta = load_meta(...) already precedes model construction; change build_model() to build_model(meta). Change the loss call to loss_fn(out, logits, yb, model.cell_u, model.cell_v). Param groups unchanged -- map_feat/map_bias fall into the existing head group at lr 1e-3 automatically. prepare_realizations, sample_epoch, calibrate_conf_shift, EPOCH_MULT=3, MIN_KEEP_RATE=0.40, batch 64, and the CLI stay byte-identical. In the info dict update the 'init' string to note the from-scratch neural-map head and add 'map_hw': [mh, mw].

=== verify before finishing (no full training) ===
10. .venv/bin/python: m = build_model(load_meta('berlin')); forward torch.zeros(2,3,128,128) -> shape [2,3], finite; export_onnx to a temp file; assert size < 4*1024*1024; run onnxruntime (CPUExecutionProvider, 1 thread) on a random uint8-derived frame -> [[u,v,conf]] finite with conf in [0,1]; time one inference and confirm well under 250 ms. Repeat the size assert with munich's meta (largest raster). If the primary conv2d export path fails any of this, switch to the fallback in step 7 and re-verify.
